decisions.accepted_answer: First cutover set is the single active private Telegram workflow only, exercised through the complete explicit P0 proof bundle. Public, household, provider, browser, and workflow fixture evidence remains in the bundle as negative/coverage proof, but no additional production workflow is included by this decision.
decisions.cutover_impact: Allows this private Telegram workflow to enter the included cutover set when all explicit proof artifacts are green.
decisions.downgrade_note: No downgrade: direct native Hermes channel credentials remain excluded; Telclaude edge/private-runtime custody remains required.

decisions.accepted_answer: Private execution must use Telclaude-owned ingress, approval, private-runtime, served-MCP, model relay, redaction, queue ownership, rollback, and network containment evidence. Raw provider/model/channel credentials in Hermes are not production evidence.
decisions.cutover_impact: Blocks cutover unless the explicit feature probes, fixtures, no-fork proof, network probes, queue snapshot, rollback rehearsal, and proof bundle validate together.
decisions.downgrade_note: No downgrade accepted for private Telegram production cutover.

decisions.accepted_answer: Generated Hermes profiles are the only Hermes-adjacent runtime artifacts accepted for this cutover. They must be generated by the Telclaude profile generator from the pinned compatibility lockfile, contain only extension/config outputs, and include no Hermes source replacement, patch, or monkeypatch artifact.
decisions.cutover_impact: Allows profile-generation proof to satisfy the no-fork/P0 cutover gate only when the run-local proof artifact validates against the pinned lockfile.
decisions.downgrade_note: No downgrade accepted for generated-profile provenance.

decisions.accepted_answer: The first private Telegram Hermes cutover does not include the chief-of-staff profile workflow. Complete-parity accounting must record that row explicitly as descoped rather than leaving it invisible.
decisions.cutover_impact: Allows the complete parity roster gate to distinguish the intentionally out-of-scope chief-of-staff row from missing production evidence; does not descope private execution, identity migration, memory, skills, redaction, or cutover safety rows.
decisions.downgrade_note: Temporary scope decision for the first private Telegram cutover; remove when a fixture.profile.chief-of-staff production fixture is added.
